title: hieracium murorum subsp. nymphaeatum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. nymphaeatum, also known as Wall hawkweed or Nymph's feather, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is used in traditional herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Hieracium murorum subsp. nymphaeatum is often made into a tea, tincture, or infused oil, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
